name: Cape Fear Regional Cdc
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor and community development organization serving low-to-moderate income residents across eight North Carolina counties with foreclosure prevention, homeownership education, and small business assistance.
description_long:
Cape Fear Regional CDC was founded in 1989 as the Wilmington-New Hanover Community Development Corporation and expanded in 2002 to serve a eight-county region including Pender, Brunswick, Onslow, Duplin, Bladen, Sampson, and Columbus Counties in addition to New Hanover. The organization operates as a non-profit focused on housing, community, and economic development with HUD certification achieved in 2002. They function as an official partner of the North Carolina Housing Finance Agency for state-funded foreclosure prevention programs and hold Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) designation from the City of Wilmington.

The organization offers comprehensive housing counseling in pre-purchase, pre-rental, homebuyer education, post-occupancy, mortgage default, and foreclosure mitigation. They administer multiple foreclosure prevention assistance programs funded through the NC Foreclosure Prevention Fund, National Foreclosure Mitigation Counseling, and Mortgage Payment Program. Beyond housing, they operate the Wilmington Business Center, a 12,000-square-foot small business incubator providing affordable office and industrial space with bundled business counseling. Additional services include housing rehabilitation programs, rental unit assistance, and development program management for local governments and lenders.

What distinguishes Cape Fear Regional CDC is their dual focus on both housing and small business development serving a geographically broader region than many community development organizations. They have demonstrated concrete impact: 25 affordable single-family homes constructed and sold to income-eligible families, over 50 businesses assisted through their business center since 1995, and active management of foreclosed properties for rehabilitation and rental. They own a 25-acre development tract in Brunswick County with plans for multi-family housing, community center, and commercial space. Their established partnerships with HUD, state housing finance agencies, and local government provide access to multiple funding streams and resources.

This organization is genuinely free and represents legitimate non-profit work, making it appropriate for the free-help category. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—services are restricted to a specific eight-county region in southeastern North Carolina. Additionally, while housing counseling is HUD-certified, the breadth of foreclosure prevention assistance depends on current funding availability from state and federal programs, which fluctuates. The organization's website lacks pricing transparency and specific details about response times or eligibility thresholds.
pros: HUD-certified housing counseling agency with multiple certifications (pre-purchase, mortgage default, foreclosure mitigation, HECM)
Official partner of NC Housing Finance Agency administering state-funded foreclosure prevention programs with no-cost assistance
Dual mission covering both housing counseling AND small business development through Wilmington Business Center incubator
Demonstrated track record: 25 homes built and sold to low-income families; 50+ businesses assisted since 1995
CHDO designation from City of Wilmington provides access to HUD CDBG and HOME program funding
Serves eight-county region (expanded from single county in 2002), covering broader geographic area than many local CDCs
Established 25-acre development tract with comprehensive site plan for affordable housing expansion
cons: Geographic limitation: services restricted to Cape Fear region (eight North Carolina counties only)
Website lacks specific details on application process, response times, wait periods, or eligibility income thresholds
Foreclosure prevention funding dependent on state and federal grant availability, which can change yearly
No online intake or application system visible; requires phone call (910.762.7555) or manual request submission
Limited information about business counseling costs or whether small business services have income-eligibility restrictions
best_for: Low-to-moderate income homeowners in Cape Fear region facing foreclosure or mortgage default
First-time homebuyers in eight-county North Carolina area seeking pre-purchase counseling and education
Small business owners/entrepreneurs in Wilmington area needing affordable incubator space and business planning assistance
Renters seeking affordable housing or guidance on rental stability in southeastern North Carolina
diagnosis: Best for low-to-moderate income residents of the Cape Fear region (eight North Carolina counties) facing housing instability, foreclosure risk, or small business startup challenges. Primary caveat: strictly geographically limited service area and funding dependent on state/federal grant cycles, so foreclosure prevention assistance availability may fluctuate.
services: Mortgage default and foreclosure mitigation counseling
Housing rehabilitation programs for low-cost home repairs
Foreclosure prevention assistance through NC state programs
Rental unit placement and pre-rental counseling
Small business incubator space (Wilmington Business Center)
Business financing identification and securing support
Development program management for local government and lenders
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
